assertions.text: V1 was traveling northbound (direction of travel) on the roadway prior to departure.
The roadway was a two-lane, two-way roadway.
V1 was negotiating a left-hand curve prior to departing the roadway.
V1 departed the roadway on the right side of the roadway.
The front plane of V1 contacted a utility pole (impact at the front of the vehicle).
V1 departed the roadway and then its front contacted a utility pole (departure occurred before impact).
There was one impact between V1 and the utility pole.
After the impact with the utility pole, V1 rotated clockwise.
V1 came to final rest in the middle of the roadway partially occupying both northbound and southbound lanes.
At final rest V1 was facing southeast (orientation of vehicle after collision).
The vehicle had sufficient force to rotate it after impact with the utility pole (implying a non-trivial collision).

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows vehicle speed values [83.0](T2) km/h at t=[-4.7](T2) s and [80.0](T2) km/h at t=[0.0](T2) s but the telemetry does not document compass or heading to confirm travel direction (northbound) so direction is unverifiable.
Roadway geometry (two-lane, two-way) is not recorded by vehicle sensors; no channel provides roadway lane-count or traffic direction, so this is unverifiable.

Steering shows a peak raw value of [27.0](T2) deg at t=[-0.2](T2) s and baseline median [4.5](T2) deg, which indicates steering input but does not by itself prove a left-hand curve versus other maneuvers because device-frame alignment to road curvature is not provided; thus negotiating a left-hand curve is unverifiable from sensors alone.
Departure side (right side of roadway) is a positional fact not captured by in-vehicle channels available; GPS track or lateral position relative to roadway is not present, so this is unverifiable.

The crash pulse shows cumulative longitudinal delta-V final value [ -75.6 ](T2) km/h with max at t=[0.24](T2) s, indicating a strong forward deceleration consistent with a frontal impact (front-plane contact) rather than rear/sidelong impact.

Sequence 'departed roadway then front contacted pole' requires external positional/timing of departure vs impact; telemetry provides pre-crash speed [83.0](T2) km/h to [80.0](T2) km/h and impact at t=[0.0](T1) but no lane-departure sensor or GPS path to confirm departure occurred before impact, so unverifiable.

EDR summary notes one prior event and the crash-pulse shows a single large longitudinal delta-V with final value [ -75.6 ](T2) km/h and a single peak at t=[0.24](T2) s, supporting there being one dominant impact recorded.

A claimed clockwise rotation after impact would require yaw/gyro or GPS heading change; available channels include delta-V longitudinal [ -75.6 ](T2) km/h and lateral delta-V final [ -6.9 ](T2) km/h but device-frame axes alignment to vehicle yaw is undocumented, so rotation direction (clockwise) is unverifiable.

Final rest position (middle of roadway occupying both lanes) is a spatial claim not recorded by onboard channels; GPS trajectory/position at rest is not available in the provided telemetry, so unverifiable.
Vehicle orientation at final rest (facing southeast) requires heading data or yaw angle at end of event; such heading is not present in the provided channels, so unverifiable.

The crash-pulse shows a large cumulative longitudinal delta-V final value [ -75.6 ](T2) km/h with EDR summary max delta-V_long [ -76.153 ](T1) km/h, indicating a high-severity impact sufficient to rotate the vehicle.

result.rationale: Telemetry provides strong evidence of a heavy frontal deceleration (longitudinal delta-V ~[ -75.6 ](T2) km/h / [ -76.153 ](T1) km/h) and a single dominant impact, but lacks GPS/heading/lane-position data to verify direction of travel, lane geometry, side of departure, rotation direction, final rest position, or final orientation.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
